title: penstemon × parishii
intro: Penstemon × parishii, also known as Parish's penstemon, is a hybrid plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant. The plant has been traditionally used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, offering health benefits such as relieving congestion and soothing sore throats. Herbal preparations of Penstemon × parishii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
